perturbation.original_text: The Company may terminate Consultant's engagement at any time by giving Consultant 60 days prior written Notice of the termination. In such case, 100% of the Consultant's unvested stock and, if applicable, option or warrant compensation of any nature will vest without any further action required on the part of the Consultant or the Company and the Company will deliver to the order of the Consultant promptly upon receipt of a written demand of the Consultant such shares of common stock or options at its sole expense as become due to Consultant hereunder. The Consultant's right to receive compensation whether in cash or securities shall survive any termination of this Agreement Without Cause.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may terminate Consultant's engagement at any time by giving Consultant 60 days prior written Notice of the termination. In such case, the vesting of the Consultant's unvested stock and, if applicable, option or warrant compensation of any nature will be determined at the Company's sole discretion and the Company will deliver shares or options, if any, after a decision has been made. The Consultant's right to receive compensation whether in cash or securities shall not automatically survive any termination of this Agreement Without Cause and shall be subject to the Company's review.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees full vesting upon termination without cause, but the modified version makes vesting discretionary. This change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original guarantee, potentially leading to disputes over compensation. Furthermore, the alteration that compensation shall not automatically survive any termination, also contradicts with the original text, where says it would survive
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without Cause. The Company may terminate Consultant's engagement at any time by giving Consultant 60 days prior written Notice of the termination. In such case, the vesting of the Consultant's unvested stock and, if applicable, option or warrant compensation of any nature will be determined at the Company's sole discretion and the Company will deliver shares or options, if any, after a decision has been made. The Consultant's right to receive compensation whether in cash or securities shall not automatically survive any termination of this Agreement Without Cause and shall be subject to the Company's review.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement regarding vesting and compensation upon termination without cause. The original text guarantees full vesting and compensation, while the changed text makes both discretionary and subject to company review. This creates a clear conflict and uncertainty, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the clause has been altered with conflicting implications from the original language. The contradicted text, which is the altered text in this case, emphasizes the company's discretion where the original assured vesting and compensation, therefore contradicting the original language that forms the basis of agreement.

perturbation.original_text: It is agreed that in the event of termination of this agreement if the Company decides that the Consultant's services are not needed during the termination period, the Company will continue to be responsible for paying cash and equity compensation as defined in Article 2 of this Agreement for the entire termination period.
perturbation.changed_text: It is agreed that in the event of termination of this agreement if the Company decides that the Consultant's services are not needed during the termination period, the Company's responsibility for paying cash and equity compensation as defined in Article 2 of this Agreement for the remaining term of termination period is at the discretion of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Company will be responsible for paying cash and equity during the termination period, while the change introduces uncertainty by making it discretionary to the company what remains of the termination period. By changing that, it does not ensure what was agreed on Article 2. The original text ensures the payment, while the modified version negates the original.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed that in the event of termination of this agreement if the Company decides that the Consultant's services are not needed during the termination period, the Company's responsibility for paying cash and equity compensation as defined in Article 2 of this Agreement for the remaining term of termination period is at the discretion of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Company's responsibility for cash and equity compensation during the termination period as defined in Article 2. The changed text introduces a contradiction by making this responsibility discretionary, thus conflicting with the original obligation and any clauses relying on the guaranteed compensation outlined in Article 2. The explanation confirms that the changed text 'negates the original'. This directly clashes with the terms of Article 2, creating a strong in-text contradiction that undermines the consultant's expected compensation.
